They were among the works of Deviants I follow, or scattered throughout the "Home" section, or even in other Deviants' posts, which proves that these blogs are important to the life of the community. I would have been proud to create them myself, if I had had the opportunity, the technical means or simply the sufficient skill or artistic eye. Don't rush, there's no hurry: take the time to look at them. I hope you enjoy them as much as I do. Ce journal rassemble quelques-unes des photos d'autres Deviants, vues ou trouvées pendant ces derniers jours, qui m'ont séduit et que j'ai placées parmi mes favoris. Elles étaient parmi les œuvres des Deviants que je suis, ou alors en vrac dans la rubrique "Home", ou encore, dans les posts d'autres Deviants, ce qui prouve que ces blogs sont importants pour la vie de la Markotxe 16 7 TOP TEN ART by Dieffi, journal TOP TEN ART 2 days ago [DEVIATION] [DEVIATION] [DEVIATION] (Till the wars are over...) Dec 10, 2024 I guess in the current world flooded with AI it's important to reach out a helping hand to other real artists. So I decided to share as many resources as I can and I hope some can find that interesting/important/useful! I'll make sure to update this periodically and keep it all "topic accurate" (as a specific sections so everybody can find something for themselves! Most of them are free to use! If you know any useful sites then feel free to share! USEFUL TOOLS FOR WRITING! Thesaurus | Synonyms and Antonyms of Words - I think this one doesn't need introduction, haha! Punpedia The Online Encyclopedia Of Puns - This one also speaks for itself! Many categories of puns at your disposal for your writing shenanigans! Insults Wiki - Please proceed with caution that it's literally a list of various insults and other types of profanity, derogatory terms! I only share it for writing purposes, I don't encourage using such against other people. Character Name Generator - A huge database of NatsuneNuko 23 88 How to draw Minty The Garden Dragon! by The-SixthLeafClover, journal How to draw Minty The Garden Dragon! Jul 1, 2024 The theme for this month is Garden Dragons! 🌱 For this tutorial I will be featuring Minty The Mint Dragon as one of my favorite Garden Dragons to draw! Follow the steps and create your own artwork! Post your drawing on DeviantArt with the tag #GardenDragonTutorial and get a Profile badge! I mainly sketch my work in Painter SAI2. A lot of my color work is done in Corel Painter using the "Mona Lisa" brush. :bulletgreen: What is a Garden Dragon? A garden dragon is a plant-like dragon that lives in a garden pot. I originally called them "Bonsai Dragons." I have evolved them since the first creation of Bonsai Dragon and have made several different variations of them like Minty Dragon. :bulletgreen: Why this art style? I love to read and make comics of my own and have developed this cute art style to fit my very own comic that I run called Lucky Dragon and Friends. I post this comic regularly here on DeviantArt as well as on WebToon. Some of the recent episodes of my comic The-SixthLeafClover 1.1K 1K BeckyKidus on DeviantArt http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/3.0/ https://www.deviantart.com/beckykidus/art/How-to-draw-realistic-art-with-colored-pencils-1071596434 BeckyKidus How to draw realistic art with colored pencils BeckyKidus 36 247 ZsoltKosa on DeviantArt https://www.deviantart.com/zsoltkosa/art/Mountain-Brush-Set-938136606 ZsoltKosa Mountain Brush Set ZsoltKosa 76 ZsoltKosa on DeviantArt https://www.deviantart.com/zsoltkosa/art/Aurora-Brush-Set-938255317 ZsoltKosa Aurora Brush Set ZsoltKosa 103 FoxRabiscos on DeviantArt https://www.deviantart.com/foxrabiscos/art/My-Brushs-for-Sketch-Lineart-and-Paint-940188258 FoxRabiscos My Brushs for Sketch Lineart and Paint FoxRabiscos 2 106 JackKiraS on DeviantArt https://www.deviantart.com/jackkiras/art/Cat-spot-brushes-1049572516 JackKiraS Cat spot brushes JackKiraS 23 258 Resources For Artists: Tutorials from Youtube 1 by phoenixleo, journal Resources For Artists: Tutorials from Youtube 1 Aug 30, 2013 Tutorials from YouTube Part 1 There are many places you will find resources to help you with your art. From deviantART's own vast resources, tutorials, stock images provided by artists to your public library, bookstores, art sessions to name a few. YouTube is one such resource hub. From live video which explains materials and products you would need for your traditional or digital artwork, to painting demonstrations that are live to 'speedpaints', they offer various ways audio-visual medium and such a large diversity of artists help with getting help you need. You are encouraged to find more and browse more to research, study and practice.... phoenixleo 45 154 Resources For Artists: Colour Scheme Tools by phoenixleo, journal Resources For Artists: Colour Scheme Tools Jan 4, 2013 Colour Scheme Tools So I have been thinking of doing a few articles apart from feature articles to link some useful resources for artists and this is the first one of these. While this is a mini one, it's helped me a lot on several occassions.Sometimes it gets confusing to decide which colours to choose. "'Should I go with a turquoise and a gold for the face? What about the room? I was going for a chic cool modern palette which is minimalistic!" Surely, a lot of us have gone through those decisive moments. Below are some colour scheme tools that would help you with your colour scheme choices. Click the titles or the links in asterisk to go... phoenixleo 87 217 Posts See All moonbeam13 Mar 4, 2026 Watch Community Events - March 2026 0 min read March is all about energy, creativity, and diving headfirst into the fun of making. From colorful photo hunts to quirky challenges, fan fiction, and tutorials that push your skills, this month invites you to experiment, explore, and celebrate your imagination. Whether you’re creating for joy, community, or a little friendly chaos, there’s something here to inspire you. [DEVIATION] ✨ Community Highlights [EMOTE]Celebrating Deviousness - March 2026 For more than 16 years, @augenweide has been a steady and inspiring presence in the photography community. His black and white landscapes are studies in patience and restraint, inviting us to slow down and really see what’s in front of us. We’re proud to celebrate him as March’s Deviousness recipient. [EMOTE] Group-Led Events [EMOTE] Palette Photo Hunt Challenge Spring is here, and so is Holi! Snap photos inspired by a free color palette, turn them into a collage, and show off your festival of colors. To help keep this space positive and focused, please remember that the comment section is not the right place to report specific deviations, issues, self-promotion, or deviants. To make sure your concerns are properly addressed, we kindly ask that you submit reports through the official support channels: DeviantArt Help Center Customer Support Report a Profile Report a Bu g Report a Deviation If you have questions or need assistance, please reach out through these official support options. Together, we can keep this community positive and inspiring for everyone. 💚 Comments that are not related to the official group may be removed.
